leagueBriefing: Dan Bradbury navigated a chaotic back nine at Houghton Golf Club to claim his second Joburg Open title, surviving a four-way battle as his rivals stumbled in the closing holes.

games.headline: Bradbury survives back-nine chaos to reclaim Joburg Open crown
games.brief: England's Dan Bradbury kept his composure during a frantic final round in South Africa to secure his third DP World Tour title. While overnight leader Hennie du Plessis collapsed on the back nine and challengers Brandon Robinson Thompson and Casey Jarvis suffered late bogeys, Bradbury carded a 5-under 65. A spectacular recovery shot from the rough on the 72nd hole set up a tap-in par, allowing Bradbury to finish at 17 under and edge out the field by a single stroke at a course he calls home away from home.
bullets: Dan Bradbury secured his second Joburg Open victory at Houghton Golf Club, adding to his 2022 maiden title.
Casey Jarvis fell just short of a historic third consecutive DP World Tour win after a bogey on the 17th and a missed birdie putt at the last.
Hennie du Plessis saw a three-shot lead evaporate following a double-bogey on the 15th and a bogey on the 16th.
Brandon Robinson Thompson held a one-shot lead heading into the final hole but a costly three-putt relegated him to a tie for second.
Bradbury's final-round 65 included seven birdies, four of which came during a clinical stretch on the back nine.
games.context: The victory significantly boosts Bradbury's pursuit of one of the ten PGA Tour cards available through the Race to Dubai rankings.
